title: oxalis pickeringii
intro: Oxalis pickeringii, also known as Pickering's sorrel or purple wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae) native to the southwestern United States. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of oxalic acid, which has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as digestive issues and rheumatism. The health benefits of Oxalis pickeringii include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. Oxalis pickeringii can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for a range of applications in herbal medicine.
